name: Quantum Fracture
author: Abdullah Al Omar Galib
description:
Quantum Fracture simulates a quantum walk on a line graph to paint jagged, crystal-fracture patterns. Each brush stroke spawns a quantum walker whose decoherence, controlled by the Measurement Rate, governs whether pixels collapse into solid colour or remain ghost-like and translucent.

Parameters:
 - Radius → thickness of each fracture arm
 - Branches → number of fracture arms radiating from the path
 - Measurement Rate → probability of collapse per step (0 = coherent quantum walk, 1 = classical random walk)
 - Colour → base hue for the fracture
 - Glow → adds a soft luminous aura around collapsed pixels

Usage:
 - Click and drag to define the spine of the fracture. Arms will branch off at quantum-determined angles.
